titlestmt.titleproper: R. Keith Dennis Mathematician Print and Photo collection8565

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-02-06 23:26:39 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: R. Keith Dennis Mathematician Print and Photo collection
did.abstract: Collection consists namely of prints, portraits and photographs of mathematicians.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: R. Keith Dennis is a Professor of Mathematics at Cornell University with a focus area on commutative and non-commutative algebra, algebraic K-theory, group theory, and mathematical bibliography.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: R. Keith Dennis Mathematician Print and Photo collection, #8565. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Collection consists namely of prints, portraits and photographs of mathematicians collected by Dennis. Notable historic figures in mathematics are represented. Several published portfolios, including editions of David Eugene Smith's Portraits of Eminent Mathematicians, containing prints and biographical information on the mathematicians are also included. Plates appear to often come from published works and in some cases include portions of text from the book from which they were removed. Also a panoramic photograph by Fenner showing the attendees of the 1946 annual meetings of the American Mathematical Society, Institute of Mathematical Statistics, and Mathematical Association of America at Cornell University, photographed in front of Barton Hall.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph by Fenner showing the attendees of the 1946 annual meetings of the American Mathematical Society, Institute of Mathematical Statistics, and Mathematical Association of America at Cornell University, photographed in front of Barton Hall.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Raymond Archibald Aristotle Isaac Barrow Wilhelm Blaschke Tycho Brahe David Brewster William Byerly Gerolamo Cardano Rudolf Clausius Nicolaus Copernicus Georges Cuvier

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Jean le Rond d'Alembert H. Ted Davis Ernest de Jonquières René Descartes Ron DiPerna Martin Eichler Epicurus Leonhard Euler Victor Felber Augustin Fresnel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Galileo Galilei Évariste Galois Carl Freidrich Gauss Sophie Germain James Joseph Glaisher Edmond Halley William Hamilton Oliver Heaviside Heinrich Hertz David Hilbert Shirley Hill Christiaan Huygens

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Fritz John Robert Kalaba Immanuel Kant Vladimir Karapetoff Johannes Kepler Cassius Keyser Felix Klein Joseph-Louis Lagrange Gabriel Lamé Pierre-Simon Laplace Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz Joseph-Louis Liouville Alfred Loewy Gino Loria

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Kurt Mahler J. C. P. Miller Simon Newcomb Isaac Newton Amalie Noether

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Georges Papy Vilfredo Pareto Blaise Pascal George Peacock Charles Peirce Michael Pupin K. G. Ramanathan Herbert Robbins

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Lao Simons David Smith William Benjamin Smith Mary Somerville Benedict Spinoza Jacob Steiner Charles Steinmetz Henry Svoboda James Joseph Sylvester Giuseppe Tallini Paul Tannery Olga Taussky-Todd John Arthur Todd Pieter Venema Alexander von Humboldt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes:Presentation of the Senior Wrangler to the Vice Chancellor , Camridge, January 1872.Trinty College A Century AgoK. H. W. Kruse, Leslie Comrie, J. T. PetersHerbert B. Dwight, Maurice Kraitchik, Horace Uhler

unittitle.title: Das projektive Zeichnen nebst den für das Zeichnen wichtigsten Aufgaben aus der ebenen Geometrie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Max Kleiber, published by Wilhelm Effenberger

unittitle.title: A Portfolio of Portraits of Eminent Mathematicians

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By David Eugene Smith, published by The Open Court Publishing Company. Incomplete set, missing several pages. Includes: Pythagoras, Pierre de Fermat, Leonardo Bonacci (2 copies), Euclid, Gerolamo Cardano, Gottfried Wilhelm Liebnitz, John Napier, François Viète, Thales of Miletus, Blaise Pascal, Johann Bernoulli (2 copies), Jakob Bernoulli, Guillaume de l'Hôpital, Bonaventura Cavalieri, Gaspard Monge, Niccolo Tartaglia, Isaac Barrow (spelled Issac).

unittitle.title: Portraits of Eminent Mathematicians Portfolio Number One

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By David Eugene Smith, puclished by Scripta Mathematica. Includes: Archimedes, Nicolaus Copernicus, François Viète, Galileo Galilei, John Napier, René Descartes, Isaac Newton (missing), Gottfried Wilhelm Liebnitz, Joseph-Louis Lagrange, Carl Freidrich Gauss (missing), Nikolai Lobachevsky, James Joseph Sylvester.

unittitle.title: Portraits of Eminent Mathematicians Portfolio Number Two

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By David Eugene Smith, puclished by Scripta Mathematica. Includes: Euclid, Gerolamo Cardano, Johannes Kepler, Pierre de Fermat (missing), Blaise Pascal, Leonhard Euler (missing), Pierre-Simon Laplace, Augustin-Louis Cauchy, Carl Gustav Jacob Jacobi, William Rowan Hamilton, Arthur Cayley (missing), Pafnuty Chebyshev, Henri Poincaré (missing).

unittitle.title: Portraits of Eminent Mathematicians Portfolio Number Two (copy 2)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By David Eugene Smith, puclished by Scripta Mathematica. Includes: Euclid (missing), Gerolamo Cardano, Johannes Kepler, Pierre de Fermat (missing), Blaise Pascal, Leonhard Euler (missing), Pierre-Simon Laplace, Augustin-Louis Cauchy, Carl Gustav Jacob Jacobi, William Rowan Hamilton, Arthur Cayley (missing), Pafnuty Chebyshev, Henri Poincaré (missing).

unittitle.title: Portraits of Famous Philosophers who were also Mathematicians

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Cassius Jackson Keyser, published by Scripta MathematicaIncludes: Pyhagoras, Plato, Aristotle, Epicurus, Roger Bacon, René Descartes, Blaise Pascal, Benedict Spinoza, Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz, George Berkeley, Immanuel Kant, Charles Peirce

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Henry Crew, published by Scripta Mathematica. Includes: Galileo, Christiaan Huygens, Isaac Nweton, André-Marie Ampère, Augustin Fresnel, Michael Faraday, James Prescott Joule, Rudolf Julius Emmanuel Clausius, James Clerk Maxwell, Josiah Willard Gibbs, Heinrich Rudolf Hertz, Henry Augustus Rowland.
